records.ety: [OE. waveren, from AS. wæfre wavering, restless. See Wave, v. i.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Wavered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Wavering.]

defs: To play or move to and fro; to move one way and the other; hence, to totter; to reel; to swing; to flutter.
quotes: With banners and pennons wavering with the wind.
Thou wouldst waver on one of these trees as a terror to all evil speakers against dignities.

defs: To be unsettled in opinion; to vacillate; to be undetermined; to fluctuate; as, to water in judgment.
quotes: Let us hold fast . . . without wavering.
In feeble hearts, propense enough before To waver, or fall off and join with idols.

defs: A sapling left standing in a fallen wood.
